2603.17145.title: REAL: Regression-Aware Reinforcement Learning for LLM-as-a-Judge
2603.17145.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as automated evaluators that assign numeric scores to model outputs, a paradigm known as LLM-as-a-Judge. However, standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods typically rely on binary rewards (e.g., 0-1 accuracy), thereby ignoring the ordinal structure inherent in regression tasks; for instance, they fail to recognize that predicting 4 is significantly better than predicting 1 when the ground truth is 5. Conversely, existing regression-aware approaches are often confined to Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), limiting their ability to explore optimal reasoning paths. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{REAL} (\underline{RE}gression-\underline{A}ware Reinforcement \underline{L}earning), a principled RL framework designed to optimize regression rewards, and also proven to be optimal for correlation metrics. A key technical challenge is that the regression objective is explicitly policy-dependent, thus invalidating standard policy gradient methods. To address this, we employ the generalized policy gradient estimator, which naturally decomposes optimization into two complementary components: (1) exploration over Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trajectory, and (2) regression-aware prediction refinement of the final score. Extensive experiments across model scales (8B to 32B) demonstrate that REAL consistently outperforms both regression-aware SFT baselines and standard RL methods, exhibiting significantly better generalization on out-of-domain benchmarks. On Qwen3-32B specifically, we achieve gains of +8.40 Pearson and +7.20 Spearman correlation over the SFT baseline, and +18.30/+11.20 over the base model. These findings highlight the critical value of integrating regression objectives into RL exploration for accurate LLM evaluation.
2603.17145.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作自動評估者，為模型輸出分配數字分數，這一範式被稱為LLM-as-a-Judge。<lb/>然而，標準的強化學習（RL）方法通常依賴於二元獎勵（例如，0-1準確率），因此忽略了回歸任務中固有的序數結構；例如，它們未能識別出當真實值為5時，預測4明顯優於預測1。<lb/>相反，現有的回歸感知方法通常僅限於監督微調（SFT），限制了它們探索最佳推理路徑的能力。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們提出了\textbf{REAL}（\underline{RE}gression-\underline{A}ware Reinforcement \underline{L}earning），這是一個旨在優化回歸獎勵的原則性RL框架，並且已被證明在相關性指標上是最佳的。<lb/>一個關鍵的技術挑戰是回歸目標顯式依賴於策略，從而使標準的策略梯度方法失效。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們採用了廣義策略梯度估計器，這自然將優化分解為兩個互補的組件：（1）在思維鏈（CoT）軌跡上的探索，以及（2）對最終分數的回歸感知預測精煉。<lb/>在不同模型規模（8B到32B）上的廣泛實驗表明，REAL始終優於回歸感知的SFT基準和標準的RL方法，在域外基準上展現出顯著更好的泛化能力。<lb/>具體而言，在Qwen3-32B上，我們在SFT基準上實現了+8.40的Pearson和+7.20的Spearman相關性增益，相較於基礎模型則為+18.30/+11.20。<lb/>這些發現突顯了將回歸目標整合到RL探索中以實現準確LLM評估的關鍵價值。
2603.17145.author: Yasi Zhang et.al.
2603.17145.authors: Yasi Zhang, Tianyu Chen, Mingyuan Zhou, Oscar Leong, Ying Nian Wu, Michal Lukasik
